title: Capa Knowledge Test
description: Test your understanding of Capa with 25 questions.

questions.question: Scenario: A centralized secrets store duplicates a test API key into production secrets during a merge, risking tenant breach. Design a beginner CAPA program to prevent recurrence? Include: 1) a CAPA data model, 2) a lifecycle state machine, 3) a minimal REST API to create/update CAPAs with evidence, 4) tenant-aware metrics to prove containment, 5) an RCA template and a canary-based preventive action to test before rollout?

Capture evidence: RBAC bindings, admission policy, namespace/tenants, and secret access logs. State machine: detect drift → contain (revoke access) → investigate → remediate → verify → close. API: RES

questions.explanation:
## Why This Is Asked
Tests ability to design CAPA for secrets risk in a multi-tenant system; beginner-level yet concrete.

## Key Concepts
- CAPA data model and lifecycle
- Evidence-driven remediation
- Metrics for containment and auditability

## Code Example
```javascript
class CAPA {
  constructor(id, description){ this.id=id; this.description=description; this.rootCause=null; this.corrective=null; this.preventive=null; this.evidence=[]; this.status='created'; }
}
```

## Follow-up Questions
- How would you audit existing CAPAs and their effectiveness?
- How would you enforce cross-tenant policy compliance across pipelines?

questions.question: Scenario: A distributed ML inference service across three regions experiences sporadic drift in input feature distributions after a schema change in the feature store, degrading model accuracy and triggering alert floods. Design a CAPA program to detect, contain, and prevent recurrence. Include: 1) CAPA data model capturing evidence, feature store version, model version, drift metrics, logs; 2) a region-aware lifecycle state machine; 3) a minimal REST API to create/update CAPAs with linked logs/traces; 4) region/drift metrics to prove containment (recurrence rate, MTTR, false positives); 5) RCA template and canary rollback plan for feature-store/schema changes?

questions.explanation:
## Why This Is Asked
Tests ability to design CAPA for ML/data drift with schema changes, across regions, not just generic incident handling.

## Key Concepts
- Data-modeling for evidence, versions, drift metrics
- Region-aware lifecycle
- Canary rollback and RCA templates
- Traceable APIs linking logs/pipelines

## Code Example
```javascript
// Minimal CAPA entity example
class CAPA { constructor(id){ this.id=id} }
```

## Follow-up Questions
- How would you automate detection of drift without labeled ground truth?
- How would you scale the API and store for large organizations?

questions.explanation:
## Why This Is Asked
New angle on CAPA: privacy/regulatory risk and cross-tenant leakage in regional logging. Tests ability to model evidence, coordinate containment across tenants, and implement canary with tenant scoping.

## Key Concepts
- Region-tenant scoping
- Data lineage and masking
- Canary and rollback
- Observability and metrics

## Code Example
```javascript
// Pseudo REST handler sketch
POST /caps
{
  "tenantId": "t123",
  "evidence": [...],
  "region": "us-west-1"
}
```

## Follow-up Questions
- How would you scale CAPA data collection to 10k tenants?
- How would you test data-leak containment offline?

questions.explanation:
## Why This Is Asked

Tests the ability to tailor CAPA for deployment-driven incidents tied to feature flags, mirroring real-world release risks.

## Key Concepts

- Incident-scoped CAPA data model
- Lightweight state machine suitable for beginners
- Minimal API design for CAPA lifecycle
- Practical metrics to prove effectiveness
- Canary-based verification strategy

## Code Example

```javascript
// Example payload for creating a CAPA (JSON)
{
  "capa_id": "CAPA-123",
  "incident_id": "INC-456",
  "flag_name": "new_home_feed",
  "region": "eu-west",
  "start_ts": 1700000000,
  "end_ts": 1700003600,
  "root_cause": "flag evaluation path added latency",
  "corrective_actions": ["roll back flag", "optimize evaluation"],
  "verification": {"canary_pass": true, "throughput_stable": true},
  "status": "Open"
}
```

## Follow-up Questions

- What fields would you deem optional to keep the CAPA lightweight, and why?
- How would you extend the data model to include evidence provenance (logs/traces) without bloating the schema?

questions.question: Scenario: In a multi-region CI/CD supply chain, a corrupted artifact is introduced into Region-1 and Region-3 private registries after a malicious PR, causing builds to pull the bad artifact and deploy a faulty feature with leaked credentials in logs. Include: 1) a CAPA data model with evidence, lineage, and owners; 2) a region-aware lifecycle (detect, contain, remediate, verify) with SLAs; 3) a minimal REST API to create/update CAPAs with logs/traces and policy links; 4) region metrics for containment and recurrence; 5) an RCA template and a canary rollout plan for dependency updates?

questions.explanation:
## Why This Is Asked
Tests ability to design enterprise-grade incident response for distributed supply chain attacks, emphasizing rapid detection, coordinated containment, and systematic prevention across multiple regions.

## Key Concepts
- CAPA data model with evidence, lineage, and ownership structures
- Region-aware incident lifecycle with strict SLA enforcement
- SBOM analysis, provenance validation, and registry quarantine procedures
- Automated rollback mechanisms and canary deployment validation
- RCA frameworks and measurable containment metrics

## Code Example
```javascript
// Example CAPA payload (POST /caps)
{
  "id": "CAPA-2026-01",
  "evidence": ["build-logs", "SBOM", "vulnerability-scans"],
  "lineage": ["artifact-A -> build-pipeline -> deployment-region1"],
  "owners": ["security-team@example.com", "sre-team@example.com"],
  "sla": {
    "detection": "15m",
    "containment": "30m",
    "remediation": "2h",
    "verification": "1h"
  }
}
```

questions.explanation:
## Why This Is Asked

Tests ability to design end‑to‑end CAPA in multi-tenant, data‑driven systems with schema drift and CDC issues. Requires data model, state machine, API, metrics, RCA, rollback; ties governance to operational reality.

## Key Concepts

- CAPA data modeling for tenants and schema lineage
- Region-aware lifecycle orchestration
- Observability with traces, logs, data lineage
- Canary governance and rollback in schema changes

## Code Example

```javascript
// example pseudo-API handler shape
POST /caps { tenant_id, source_system, schema_version, evidence_refs }
```

## Follow-up Questions

- How would you test MTTR and drift detection statistically?
- How do you prevent future leakage via authorization controls?

Tests ability to design end-to-end CAPA with data lineage, regional governance, and runtime controls for notebook-driven data work.

```javascript
}
```

## Follow-up Questions
- How would you implement deterministic alert correlation across regions?
- What would you monitor to minimize false positives?

questions.explanation:
## Why This Is Asked
Tests ability to design end-to-end CAPA for data privacy in a multi-tenant ML data plane, not just generic fixes.

## Key Concepts
- CAPA data model with evidence, lineage, owners
- Privacy-first validation, PII masking, DLP signals
- Tenant-aware lifecycle with SLAs and canary rollout

## Code Example
```javascript
// Example CAPA schema sketch
const capa = { id: '', tenantId: '', evidence: [], lineage: [], owners: [], status: '', timestamps: {} }
```

## Follow-up Questions
- How would you quantify containment time and recurrence? 
- How integrate with audit logs and policy docs?

questions.explanation:
## Why This Is Asked
Tests end-to-end CAPA design for ML-driven data quality issues in real-time pipelines, requiring evidence collection, lineage, ownership, and a rollback/canary strategy.

## Key Concepts
- CAPA data model with evidence and lineage
- Drift detection and quarantine mechanisms
- Lifecycle: detect, contain, remediate, verify with measurable SLAs
- Minimal REST API for CAPA management with logs/traces and policy links
- RCA templates and canary rollout plans for model updates

## Code Example
```javascript
// Example CAPA payload structure
{
  "id": "CAPA-2026-04-01-001",
  "evidence": {"logs": ["log1", "log2"], "modelVersion": "v1.2.3", "timestamp": "2026-04-01T12:00:00Z"},
  "lineage": ["featureA", "jobX"],
  "owners": ["data-eng@example.com"]
}
```

## Follow-up Questions
- How would you measure the effectiveness of the CAPA program over time?
- What changes would you make if data freshness windows shift and drift thresholds require tuning?

## Why This Is Asked
Assesses ability to design a practical, beginner CAPA plan for data integrity across regions.

## Key Concepts
- CAPA data model fields and traceability
- Lifecycle state machine for containment and verification
- Evidence handling and regional metrics

```javascript
// Example CAPA payload
{
  "id":"CAPA-1003",

}
```

- How would you validate the canary remediation before wider rollout?
- What logs and traces would you collect to support RCA and postmortems?

## Why This Is Asked


- What signals would trigger an automatic rollback during canary rollout?

questions.explanation:
## Why This Is Asked

Tests ability to design a governance loop around a real-time data-misbehavior incident, focusing on data masking integrity, cross-region variance, and actionable remediation.

## Key Concepts

- Real-time masking integrity and race-condition risk
- Data provenance: evidence, lineage, ownership
- Region-aware lifecycle with SLAs
- Lightweight CAPA management API
- Metrics: leakage rate, MTTR, containment time, recurrence

## Code Example

```javascript
// Minimal CAPA data model sketch
const CAPA = {
  id: 'capa-123',
  title: 'Masking race-condition leak in APAC',
  evidence: ['log1', 'log2'],
  lineage: { source: 'stream-A', transform: 'maskerV2' },
  owners: ['sec-team', 'data-eng'],
  region: 'APAC',
  state: 'detected',
  sla: '24h'
};
```

## Follow-up Questions

- How would you measure and alert on leakage rate and MTTR across regions?
- What tests would you add to validate the canary rollout plan before global deployment?

questions.explanation:
## Why This Is Asked

Tests ability to design end-to-end CAPA workflow for privacy incidents, including across tenants and regions, with concrete artifacts and controls.

## Key Concepts

- CAPA data model, state machine, API design, region-tenant metrics, RCA, canary rollback, policy gates.

## Code Example

```javascript
// Example CAPA payload shape
interface CAPA { id: string; tenants: string[]; regions: string[]; evidence_refs: string[]; artifacts: string[]; state: string; severity: string; timestamps: Record<string,string>; }
```

## Follow-up Questions

- How would you verify redact coverage? What tests would you add?
- How do you design region-aware dashboards to avoid alert fatigue?

questions.explanation:
## Why This Is Asked
This question evaluates practical CAPA design skills for data leakage in multi-region real-time pipelines. It emphasizes evidence capture, data lineage tracking, clear ownership assignment, and measurable containment with region-specific SLAs.

## Key Concepts
- CAPA data model with evidence and lineage
- Region-aware lifecycle with defined SLAs
- API design for creating and updating CAPAs with audit logs and traces
- Canary deployment and rollback strategies for schema or policy changes
- Regional metrics for containment effectiveness and recurrence tracking

## Evaluation Criteria
Strong answers demonstrate understanding of end-to-end CAPA lifecycle management, including how to detect and contain incidents quickly while maintaining audit trails. Candidates should illustrate familiarity with data lineage tracking across regions, SLA enforcement, and automated rollback mechanisms. The ability to design measurable containment and recurrence metrics shows maturity in operational security practices.

- CAPA data model with evidence, redaction config, tenant IDs, and logs

questions.explanation:
## Why This Question Matters

This question tests your ability to handle **data governance in distributed systems**. Specifically, it checks your understanding of:

### Core Concepts Covered

1. **CAPA (Corrective and Preventive Action)**
   - A structured approach to handling security incidents
   - Must track evidence, lineage, and owners
   - Has clear lifecycle phases with deadlines

2. **Multi-Region Architecture**
   - Different regions may have different configurations
   - A change safe in one region may break another
   - Need regional kill switches for quick rollback

3. **Incident Response Lifecycle**
   - Detect → Contain → Remediate → Verify → Prevent
   - Each phase has specific SLAs
   - Must work differently for affected vs. unaffected regions

4. **Safe Deployment Practices**
   - Canary releases test changes on small traffic first
   - Metrics must validate before expanding
   - Automatic rollback on failure

5. **Observability**
   - Trace IDs for request tracking
   - Region-specific metrics
   - Evidence collection for audits

### What Makes a Good Answer
- Clear data model with all required fields
- Region-specific handling (not one-size-fits-all)
- Measurable SLAs
- Practical API design
- Actionable RCA template
- Safe rollout process with rollback capability

questions.explanation:
## Why This Is Asked
Candiates show ability to model CAPA data for privacy incidents and build testable containment strategies.

## Key Concepts
- CAPA data model with evidence mapping
- Region-aware containment metrics
- RCA template and canary remediation

## Code Example
```javascript
// Minimal CAPA payload example
const capa = {
  incidentId: 'INC-001',
  region: 'us-west-1',
  asset: 'export-job-logs',
  exposure: 'PII',
  evidence: ['https://evidence.example/log1'],
  rootCause: 'misconfigured export',
  containment: 'paused export',
  preventiveActions: ['policy review'],
  timestamps: { detected: '2026-01-01T00:00:00Z' }
}
```

## Follow-up Questions
- How would you verify MTTC improvements after remediation?
- What changes would you make to prevent similar exposures in the future?

## Why This Is Asked


## Follow-up Questions
- How would you extend the model for multi-tenant isolation?
- What thresholds would you set for auto-escalation of CAPAs?

questions.explanation:
## Why This Is Asked
Tests ability to design end‑to‑end CAPA for privacy/compliance risk in a multi‑tenant data export workflow, with cross‑region containment.

## Key Concepts
- CAPA data model with evidence, policy versions, and artifacts
- Region/tenant-aware lifecycle and metrics
- REST API for linked evidence; RCA templates
- Canary rollout for policy updates

## Code Example
```typescript
type CAPA = {
  id: string
  tenantId: string
  region: string
  state: 'open'|'investigating'|'contained'|'rootCause'|'remedied'|'verified'|'closed'
  createdAt: string
  updatedAt: string
  evidence: { exportId: string; logs: string; redactionVersion: string }[]
  artifacts: string[]
}
```

## Follow-up Questions
- How would you scale the evidence store for high cardinality tenants?
- How would you safeguard against metadata leakage in logs during RCA?

questions.explanation:
## Why This Is Asked
Tests the ability to architect an end-to-end CAPA workflow for a data-platform reliability incident that spans regions and tenants, including how to model evidence and link traces for audit trails.

## Key Concepts
- CAPA data model with evidence and linked artifacts
- Lifecycle state machine for CAPA progression
- Minimal REST API design for creating/updating CAPAs
- Region- and tenant-aware metrics (recurrence, MTTR, false positives)
- RCA templates and canary rollout planning

## Code Example
```javascript
// Example CAPA schema (illustrative)
{
  id: string,
  title: string,
  region: string,
  tenant_id: string,
  status: string,
  evidence: string[],
  artifacts: string[],
  remediation: object
}
```

## Follow-up Questions
- How would you automate evidence collection across services?
- How would you handle new tenants with no historical CAPA data?

questions.explanation:
## Why This Is Asked

Tests ability to model data-centric CAPAs across regions, focusing on data quality, traceability, and safe rollback.

## Key Concepts

- CAPA data model with evidence, rootCause, containment, remediation
- Lifecycle state machine for cross-region issues
- Idempotent REST API design with evidence payloads
- Region-aware metrics (time to containment, duplicate rate)
- RCA templates and canary-based remediation plans

## Code Example

```javascript
// Minimal CAPA schema sketch
const CAPA = {
  id: '',
  region: '',
  evidenceLinks: [],
  rootCause: '',
  containmentActions: [],
  remediationPlan: '',
  status: '',
  timestamps: {}
};
```

## Follow-up Questions

- How would you ensure idempotency in the API?
- What tests would validate the canary rollout?
